L1.area: Logging infrastructure and its various output transport backends

L2.description: A cryptographic weakness exists where TLS certificate verification is unconditionally disabled in a network transport configuration, allowing man-in-the-middle attacks to intercept or modify traffic. An attacker in a privileged network position could exploit this to read or alter sensitive data in transit.

L3.area: Email notification transport within the logging subsystem
L3.description: A cryptographic weakness exists where TLS certificate verification is unconditionally disabled when establishing secure connections for email-based log notification delivery. An attacker in a privileged network position could intercept or modify email traffic containing sensitive log data via a man-in-the-middle attack.

ground_truth.reason: The SMTP log writer hardcodes InsecureSkipVerify to true in its TLS configuration, unconditionally disabling server certificate validation and enabling man-in-the-middle attacks on email log delivery.
